# Estimating Total Human Window-Gazing in 1800

## Key Parameters

| Parameter | Estimate | Reasoning |
|-----------|----------|-----------|
| **World population** | ~950 million | Well-established historical estimate |
| **Fraction with window access** | ~50% (475M people) | Europe (~80%), China (~60%, paper/wooden windows), India (~30%), Africa (~25%), etc. Many dwellings worldwide had some form of window opening |
| **Average gazing time per day** (for those with windows) | ~5 minutes | Most people were outdoor agricultural laborers; indoor time during daylight ~2–3 hrs; a few weather-checks, brief glances, and domestic pauses near windows add up |

## Calculation

$$475{,}000{,}000 \text{ people} \times 5 \text{ min/day} \times 365 \text{ days} \div 1{,}440 \text{ min/day}$$

$$= \frac{866{,}875{,}000{,}000 \text{ person-minutes}}{1{,}440} \approx 602{,}000{,}000 \text{ person-days}$$

## Key considerations
- Evening hours contribute little (no electric light → can't see outside)
- Women, children, and elderly spent more time indoors, offsetting field laborers
- Even brief repeated glances (10–15 per day × 20–30 sec) reach ~5 min easily

## My estimate

**600,000,000 person-days**

| Americas & other | ~110M | ~30% | 33M |
| **Total** | | | **~450M** |

- Infants: ~0 min/day

## My Estimate

# Estimating Total Person-Days Spent Looking Through Windows in 1800

## Key Parameters

**World population (1800):** ~1 billion

**Fraction with meaningful window access (~45%):**
| Region | Population | Window access rate | People with windows |
|---|---|---|---|
| China | 300M | ~65% (paper windows common) | 195M |
| Europe | 200M | ~65% (glass/shuttered) | 130M |
| India/S. Asia | 200M | ~30% | 60M |
| Sub-Saharan Africa | 100M | ~10% | 10M |
| SE Asia | 80M | ~20% | 16M |
| Japan/Korea | 40M | ~50% | 20M |
| Middle East/N. Africa | 50M | ~45% | 23M |
| Americas | 30M | ~25% | 8M |
| **Total** | | | **~450M** |

**Average daily window-gazing time (~4.5 minutes/day):**
- Most people were agricultural laborers, outdoors most of the day
- Checking weather, watching approaching visitors, idle gazing
- Higher in winter (more indoor time), lower in summer
- Children, elderly, wealthier people gaze more; laborers less

## Calculation

$$450{,}000{,}000 \times 4.5 \text{ min/day} \times 365 \text{ days} \div 1440 \text{ min/day}$$

$$= 513{,}281{,}250 \text{ person-days}$$

**World population in 1800:** ~1 billion

| South Asia | ~190M | ~35% | ~67M |
| Middle East/N. Africa | ~30M | ~50% | ~15M |
| Sub-Saharan Africa | ~100M | ~15% | ~15M |
| Americas | ~30M | ~35% | ~10M |
| **Total** | | | **~440–480M** |

**Average daily minutes looking through a window:**
- Most people (~80%) were agricultural workers spending daylight outdoors → ~3–4 min/day
- Urban/craft workers → ~10–15 min/day
- Weighted average across all window-havers: **~4.5 minutes/day**

## Calculation

$$460{,}000{,}000 \;\text{people} \times 4.5 \;\text{min/day} \times 365 \;\text{days} \div 1{,}440 \;\text{min/day}$$

$$= \frac{755{,}700{,}000{,}000}{1{,}440} \approx 524{,}800{,}000 \;\text{person-days}$$

## My Estimate

**550,000,000 person-days**

## My estimate: **550,000,000 days**

## Key Parameters

**World population in 1800:** ~1 billion

- China (~330M): ~50% (paper/lattice windows common) → ~165M

- **Total: ~450–500 million people**

**Average daily window-gazing time (~4.5 min/day):**

# Estimating Total Human Window-Gazing Days in 1800

## Key Parameters

**World population in 1800:** ~1 billion

**Fraction with access to windows (broadly defined, including unglazed openings):**
- Europe (~200M): ~80% → 160M
- China (~330M): ~40% (lattice/paper windows, openings) → 130M
- India (~200M): ~25% → 50M
- Japan (~30M): ~50% → 15M
- Americas (~30M settled): ~50% → 15M
- Middle East/Africa/Other: ~30M
- **Total: ~400 million people**

**Average daily window-gazing time (~7 minutes/day):**
- ~85% agricultural workers: ~5 min/day (mostly outdoors; brief weather/daylight checks)
- ~10% urban/indoor workers & domestic women: ~12 min/day
- ~5% elderly, wealthy, confined indoors: ~20 min/day
- **Weighted average: ≈7 minutes/day**

## Calculation

$$400{,}000{,}000 \;\text{people} \times 7\;\text{min/day} \times 365\;\text{days} \div 1440\;\text{min/day}$$

$$= 400{,}000{,}000 \times 1.774 \approx 709{,}700{,}000$$

## My Estimate

**$\boxed{710{,}000{,}000}$ person-days**

This reflects that while most of humanity was outdoors working, hundreds of millions still glanced through windows daily — checking weather, watching roads, or idly gazing — averaging a few minutes each.

# Estimating Total Window-Gazing Time in 1800

## Key Parameters

### World Population in 1800
Well-established at roughly **1 billion**.

### Fraction with Access to Windows (~43%)
| Region | Population | % with windows | People with windows |
|--------|-----------|---------------|-------------------|
| Europe | ~200M | ~60% | 120M |
| China | ~330M | ~50% (paper/lattice) | 165M |
| India | ~170M | ~30% | 51M |
| Japan | ~30M | ~50% | 15M |
| Middle East | ~30M | ~40% | 12M |
| Africa | ~100M | ~15% | 15M |
| Americas/Other | ~140M | ~20% | 28M |
| **Total** | | | **~430 million** |

### Average Daily Window-Gazing Time (~4.5 minutes)
- **Adult men** (mostly outdoor laborers): ~2 min/day
- **Adult women** (more indoor domestic work, checking on children, watching for visitors): ~8 min/day
- **Children** (~40% of population): ~3 min/day
- **Elderly/infirm**: ~15 min/day

Weighted average ≈ **4.5 minutes/day** — noting there was no TV/internet, but most people were extremely busy with manual labor.

## Calculation

$$430{,}000{,}000 \text{ people} \times 4.5 \text{ min/day} \times 365 \text{ days} \div 1{,}440 \text{ min/day}$$

$$= \frac{430{,}000{,}000 \times 1{,}642.5}{1{,}440} \approx 490{,}000{,}000 \text{ days}$$

## My Estimate

$$\boxed{490{,}000{,}000 \text{ days}}$$

# Estimating Total Human Window-Gazing in 1800

## Key Parameters

**World population in 1800:** ~1,000,000,000

**Fraction with access to some form of window (~40%):**
- Europe (~200M): ~65% had glass or shuttered windows → 130M
- China (~325M): ~50% had paper/lattice windows → 163M
- India/S. Asia (~200M): ~30% had window openings → 60M
- Sub-Saharan Africa (~90M): ~15% → 14M
- Americas, Middle East, SE Asia (~185M): ~25% → 33M
- **Total: ~400 million people**

**Average daily time looking through a window: ~7 minutes/day**
- Most people were outdoor agricultural laborers — limited indoor time
- Typical indoor activities (meals, evening rest, indoor crafts) might yield brief glances
- Weather-checking, idle gazing during rest periods

# Estimating Total Human Window-Gazing in 1800

## Key Parameters

| Parameter | Estimate | Reasoning |
|-----------|----------|-----------|
| **World population (1800)** | ~1,000,000,000 | Well-established historical estimate |
| **Fraction with window access** | ~40% | Glass windows mainly in Europe/colonial settlements (~150M); paper/lattice windows in East Asia (~130M); open shuttered openings in South Asia, Middle East, etc. (~120M). Many tropical/Sub-Saharan dwellings lacked clear windows. |
| **Avg. daily gazing time (those with access)** | ~4.5 minutes | Most people were agricultural laborers spending daylight hours *outdoors*. Indoor time was for meals, chores, sleep. Window-gazing = brief weather checks, occasional glances, some idle contemplation. Weighted down by children and heavy laborers; weighted up by elderly, craftsmen, and the leisure class. |

## Calculation

$$400{,}000{,}000 \text{ people} \times 365 \text{ days} \times \frac{4.5 \text{ min}}{1440 \text{ min/day}}$$

$$= 400{,}000{,}000 \times 365 \times 0.003125$$

$$\approx 456{,}250{,}000 \text{ person-days}$$

## My Point Estimate

$$\boxed{450{,}000{,}000 \text{ days}}$$

This reflects genuine uncertainty — modest window access across the developing world and very limited leisure time for deliberate gazing in a pre-industrial society. The number could plausibly range from ~350M to ~550M depending on how generously one defines "window" and "looking through," but **~450 million days** is my honest best central estimate.

# Estimating Total Human Window-Gazing in 1800

## Key Parameters

**World population in 1800:** ~1 billion

**People with meaningful window access (~40%):** ~400 million
- **Europe (~200M):** ~70% had glass/proper windows → 140M
- **China (~300M):** Paper lattice windows were widespread → 150M
- **India/South Asia (~200M):** Many homes had openings, but fewer proper windows → 60M
- **Japan/Korea (~35M):** Paper windows common → 17M
- **Middle East (~40M):** ~40% → 16M
- **Sub-Saharan Africa, Americas, other:** ~20M combined

**Average daily window-gazing time: ~5 minutes/day**
| Group | Share | Minutes/day |
|---|---|---|
| Farmers/laborers (outdoors most of day) | ~60% | ~2–3 |
| Urban artisans/workers | ~15% | ~6 |
| Domestic workers/housewives | ~10% | ~8 |
| Children (indoors sometimes) | ~10% | ~4 |
| Elderly, wealthy, sick | ~5% | ~15 |
| **Weighted average** | | **~5 min** |

## Calculation

$$400{,}000{,}000 \;\text{people} \times 5 \;\text{min/day} \times 365 \;\text{days} \div 1{,}440 \;\text{min/day}$$

$$= \frac{730{,}000{,}000{,}000}{1{,}440} \approx 507{,}000{,}000 \;\text{days}$$

## My estimate: **507,000,000 days**

# Estimating Total Window-Gazing Time in 1800

## Key Parameters

**World population in 1800:** ~1,000,000,000

**Fraction with meaningful window access (~45%):**
- Europe (~200M): ~60% had glass or usable windows
- China (~300M): paper/lattice windows common; ~40%
- India (~200M): openings/shutters varied; ~30%
- Americas, Africa, rest: varied; ~20–40%
- Weighted average: ~45% → **450,000,000 people**

**Average daily window-looking time (~5 minutes/day for those with access):**
- Most people were agricultural laborers, outdoors 10–14 hours/day
- Waking indoor time: ~2–6 hours (rest was sleep)
- During indoor time: checking weather, watching for visitors, brief glances while doing domestic tasks
- Wealthy/urban individuals: higher (~10–15 min); laborers: lower (~2–4 min)
- Weighted average for those with windows: **~5 minutes/day**

## Calculation

$$450{,}000{,}000 \text{ people} \times \frac{5}{1440} \text{ days/person/day} \times 365 \text{ days/year}$$

$$= 450{,}000{,}000 \times 0.00347 \times 365 \approx 570{,}000{,}000$$

## My Estimate

**570,000,000 days**

# Estimating Total Window-Gazing Days in 1800

## Key Parameters

**World population in 1800:** ~950 million

**Fraction with access to windows (openings to look through):**
| Region | Population | Window access rate | People with access |
|--------|-----------|-------------------|-------------------|
| China | ~300M | ~65% | ~195M |
| Europe | ~200M | ~80% | ~160M |
| India | ~200M | ~45% | ~90M |
| Japan | ~30M | ~60% | ~18M |
| Sub-Saharan Africa | ~80M | ~18% | ~14M |
| Rest of world | ~140M | ~35% | ~49M |
| **Total** | | | **~525 million** |

**Average daily time looking through a window (~4.5 minutes/day):**
- Working adults (mostly outdoor labor, brief morning/mealtime glances): ~3 min/day
- Women doing domestic work indoors: ~7 min/day
- Elderly/sick/homebound: ~10 min/day
- Children: ~5 min/day
- Bad weather days boost the average slightly

## Calculation

$$525{,}000{,}000 \text{ people} \times 4.5 \text{ min/day} \times 365 \text{ days} = 862{,}312{,}500{,}000 \text{ person-minutes}$$

$$\frac{862{,}312{,}500{,}000}{1{,}440 \text{ min/day}} \approx 599{,}000{,}000 \text{ days}$$

## My estimate: **600,000,000 days**

answer:
# Estimating Total Human Window-Gazing in 1800

## Key Parameters

**World population in 1800:** ~1 billion

**Fraction with meaningful window access (~40%):** ~400 million
- Europe (~200M): most dwellings had glass or shuttered windows → ~160M
- East Asia (~350M): widespread paper/lattice windows → ~175M
- South Asia, Americas, Middle East, Africa: lower rates → ~65M combined

**Average daily window-gazing time: ~10 minutes/day**
- Most people were agricultural laborers spending 10+ hours outdoors
- Indoor activities included checking weather, watching roads for visitors, supervising children — multiple brief glances
- No modern entertainment → window-gazing was a genuine idle pastime
- Winter months meant more indoor time; small/sparse windows limited gazing
- Balancing heavier gazers (elderly, children, wealthier urbanites) against field laborers

## Calculation

$$400{,}000{,}000 \;\text{people} \;\times\; 365 \;\text{days} \;\times\; \frac{10}{1{,}440} \;\text{(day-fraction per day)}$$

$$= 400{,}000{,}000 \times 2.535 \approx 1{,}014{,}000{,}000$$

## My Estimate

**1,000,000,000 days** (1 billion person-days)

This comfortably exceeds the 500,000,000 threshold. Even conservative adjustments (e.g., 300M people, 7 min/day) yield ~530 million, still above it.

# Estimating Total Human Window-Gazing Time in 1800

## Key Parameters

**World population in 1800:** ~950 million

**Fraction with meaningful window access:**
| Region | Population | % with windows | People with access |
|---|---|---|---|
| Europe | ~195M | ~65% | ~127M |
| China | ~330M | ~45% (paper/lattice) | ~149M |
| Japan/Korea | ~35M | ~50% (shoji screens) | ~18M |
| Indian subcontinent | ~185M | ~25% | ~46M |
| Middle East/C. Asia | ~35M | ~35% | ~12M |
| SE Asia, Africa, Americas | ~170M | ~10% | ~17M |
| **Total** | | | **~370–400M** |

I'll use **400 million** with window access.

**Average daily time looking through a window:**
- ~70% agricultural laborers (outdoors most of the day): **~4 min/day**
- ~15% urban workers: **~8 min/day**
- ~5% middle/upper class (more leisure): **~20 min/day**
- ~10% young children: **~3 min/day**

Weighted average: **~5.5 minutes/day**

## Calculation

$$400{,}000{,}000 \;\text{people} \times \frac{5.5}{1440}\;\text{days/day} \times 365\;\text{days/year}$$

$$= 400{,}000{,}000 \times 1.394 \approx 558{,}000{,}000\;\text{person-days}$$

## My estimate: **550,000,000 days**

# Estimating Total Human Window-Gazing Days in 1800

## Key Parameters

**World Population (1800):** ~1 billion

**Fraction with meaningful window access:**
| Region | Population | % with windows | People with access |
|--------|-----------|---------------|-------------------|
| Europe | ~200M | ~70% | 140M |
| East Asia | ~400M | ~55% | 220M |
| South Asia | ~200M | ~35% | 70M |
| Americas | ~30M | ~30% | 9M |
| Middle East | ~30M | ~40% | 12M |
| Africa | ~100M | ~15% | 15M |
| SE Asia | ~40M | ~25% | 10M |
| **Total** | | | **~476M** |

*Note: Includes glass, paper, lattice, and open-shutter windows — all common in their respective architectural traditions.*

**Average daily time looking through a window: ~4.5 minutes**
- Most people were agricultural, spending much daylight outdoors
- But when indoors, windows were the *only* light source — tasks were done beside them, with frequent outward glances
- Weighted across heavy gazers (urban, elderly, homebound) and minimal gazers (field laborers, infants)

## Calculation

$$476{,}000{,}000 \;\text{people} \times \frac{4.5}{1440}\;\text{day/day} \times 365\;\text{days} \approx \boxed{480{,}000{,}000}$$

**My best estimate: 480,000,000 days.**

# Estimating Total Window-Gazing in 1800

## Key Parameters

**World population in 1800:** ~1 billion

**People with access to windows (any type — glass, paper, lattice, open shuttered openings):**
| Region | Population | % with windows | People with windows |
|--------|-----------|---------------|-------------------|
| Europe | ~200M | ~80% | 160M |
| China | ~330M | ~60% | 200M |
| India/South Asia | ~200M | ~40% | 80M |
| Japan/SE Asia | ~70M | ~40% | 28M |
| Middle East/N. Africa | ~40M | ~50% | 20M |
| Sub-Saharan Africa | ~100M | ~20% | 20M |
| Americas | ~30M | ~40% | 12M |
| **Total** | | | **~530M** |

**Average daily window-gazing time (for those with windows): ~3.5 minutes/day**
- Most people were outdoor laborers, limiting indoor time
- But windows were the *only* light source — people worked near them (sewing, cooking, reading) and naturally glanced out
- Includes checking weather, watching for visitors, children daydreaming
- Wealthy/elderly/children skew higher (~10 min); laborers skew lower (~1–2 min)

## Calculation

$$530{,}000{,}000 \times 3.5 \;\text{min/day} \times 365 \;\text{days} \div 1{,}440 \;\text{min/day}$$

$$= \approx 470{,}000{,}000 \;\text{person-days}$$

## My estimate: **470,000,000 days**

answer:
# Estimating Total Window-Gazing Days in 1800

## Key Parameters

**World population in 1800:** ~1 billion

**Fraction with access to some form of window (~65%):**
- Europe (~200M): ~85% had glass/glazed windows
- China (~330M): ~60% (paper/lattice windows common)
- India (~170M): ~50% (openings, limited glass)
- Africa (~100M): ~25%
- Rest of world: ~50%

→ **~650 million** people with window access

**Excluding infants/toddlers (<3 yrs):** Remove ~10% → **~585 million** active window-gazers

**Average daily window-gazing time (~3.5 minutes/day):**
| Group | % of pop. | Avg. minutes/day |
|---|---|---|
| Men (mostly outdoor labor) | 40% | 2 |
| Women (more indoor work: cooking, sewing, childcare) | 40% | 5 |
| Children (3–15) | 15% | 3 |
| Elderly | 5% | 6 |
| **Weighted average** | | **~3.55 min/day** |

## Calculation

$$585{,}000{,}000 \;\times\; 3.5 \;\text{min/day} \;\times\; 365 \;\text{days} \;\div\; 1{,}440 \;\text{min/day}$$

$$= 585{,}000{,}000 \times 0.887 \;\approx\; 519{,}000{,}000$$

## My estimate: **530,000,000 days**

This accounts for seasonal variation (longer indoor time in northern winters) and the fact that even brief habitual glances (checking weather, watching for arrivals, idle gazing during chores) accumulate meaningfully across hundreds of millions of people over a full year.

# Estimating Total Window-Gazing Days in 1800

## Key Parameters

### World Population
~1,000,000,000 (well-established)

### Fraction with Access to Windows
| Region | Population | % with windows | People with windows |
|--------|-----------|---------------|-------------------|
| Europe (~200M) | Glass windows widespread | ~75% | 150M |
| China (~300M) | Paper/lattice windows common | ~60% | 180M |
| South Asia (~200M) | Openings in many homes | ~40% | 80M |
| Americas (~30M) | Mixed | ~50% | 15M |
| Africa (~100M) | Many basic dwellings | ~20% | 20M |
| Middle East/Other (~170M) | Mixed | ~35% | ~65M |

**Total ≈ 500 million people**

### Average Daily Window-Gazing Time (for those with windows)
- **Outdoor workers (~60%):** ~3 min/day (they're mostly outside)
- **Indoor workers/homemakers (~20%):** ~10 min/day
- **Children indoors (~10%):** ~5 min/day
- **Elderly/infirm (~10%):** ~15 min/day

**Weighted average ≈ 5.5 minutes/day**

## Calculation

$$500{,}000{,}000 \times 5.5 \text{ min/day} \times 365 \text{ days} \div 1440 \text{ min/day}$$

$$= \boxed{697{,}000{,}000 \text{ days}}$$

**My point estimate: 700,000,000 days**

# Estimating Total Window-Gazing in 1800

## Key Parameters

**World population in 1800:** ~1 billion

**Fraction with meaningful window access (~45–50%):**
- Europe (~200M): ~80% had windows → 160M
- China (~330M): ~70% had lattice/paper windows → 230M
- India (~200M): ~50% had window openings → 100M
- Middle East/Japan/Korea: ~55M
- Africa, Americas, SE Asia: ~30M
- **Total: ~475 million people**

**Average daily time spent looking through a window (~3.5 min/day):**
Most people were agricultural and spent the bulk of daylight outdoors. Weighting by lifestyle:
| Group | % of window-havers | Minutes/day |
|---|---|---|
| Agricultural workers | 60% | ~1.5 |
| Domestic/home workers | 20% | ~5 |
| Urban artisans | 10% | ~4 |
| Children | 5% | ~3 |
| Elderly/infirm | 5% | ~8 |

**Weighted average ≈ 3.5 minutes/day**

## Calculation

$$475{,}000{,}000 \;\text{people} \times 365 \;\text{days} \times \frac{3.5 \;\text{min}}{1440 \;\text{min/day}}$$

$$= 421{,}440{,}972 \;\text{person-days}$$

## My Estimate

**≈ 420,000,000 days**

# Estimating Total Human Window-Gazing in 1800

## Key Parameters

**World population in 1800:** ~1 billion

**Fraction with access to windows (broadly defined — glass, paper, open lattice/shutters):**
| Region | Population | % with windows | People |
|---|---|---|---|
| Europe | 200M | ~75% | 150M |
| China | 330M | ~40% | 132M |
| Indian subcontinent | 170M | ~30% | 51M |
| Japan | 30M | ~40% | 12M |
| Middle East/N. Africa | 30M | ~50% | 15M |
| Americas | 55M | ~25% | 14M |
| SE Asia/Other | 95M | ~20% | 19M |
| Sub-Saharan Africa | 90M | ~15% | 14M |
| **Total** | | | **~407M** |

**Average daily time looking through a window:**
- Farmers (~60%): ~2 min/day (check weather, brief glances)
- Indoor domestic workers, urban dwellers (~30%): ~8 min/day (frequent glances while working)
- Elderly/children/sick (~10%): ~5 min/day

**Weighted average ≈ 4.5 minutes/day**

## Calculation

$$407{,}000{,}000 \;\times\; \frac{4.5}{1440} \;\times\; 365 \;\approx\; 464{,}000{,}000 \text{ days}$$

## My estimate: **470,000,000 days**

## Key Parameters

**World population in 1800:** ~1 billion

- Most people were agricultural laborers spending the majority of daylight hours *outdoors*
- Indoor time (meals, rest, evening) might total 2–4 waking hours
- During indoor time, occasional glances through windows, positioning near windows for light (no electricity), checking weather/livestock
- Seasonal variation: more in winter, less in summer
- ~6 min/day ≈ 3–5% of non-sleeping indoor time — reasonable for a pre-electric era

## Calculation

$$325{,}000{,}000 \;\text{people} \times 6 \;\text{min/day} \times 365 \;\text{days} \div 1{,}440 \;\text{min/day}$$

$$= \frac{325{,}000{,}000 \times 2{,}190}{1{,}440}$$

$$\approx 494{,}000{,}000 \;\text{person-days}$$

## My estimate: **490,000,000 days**

**World population in 1800:** ~1 billion

| Women (domestic/indoor work) | 25% | ~8 min |
| Children | 35% | ~3 min |
| Elderly/infirm | 10% | ~8 min |

$$500{,}000{,}000 \;\text{people} \times 5 \;\text{min/day} \times 365 \;\text{days} \div 1440 \;\text{min/day} \approx 634{,}000{,}000 \;\text{days}$$

## My Estimate

**630,000,000 days**

# Estimating Total Human Window-Gazing in 1800

## Key Parameters

**World population in 1800:** ~1 billion

**Fraction with meaningful window access:**
- Europe (~200M): ~80% → 160M
- China (~330M): ~60% → 198M (paper/lattice windows common)
- South Asia (~200M): ~40% → 80M
- Japan/Korea (~35M): ~60% → 21M
- Middle East (~30M): ~50% → 15M
- Americas (~30M): ~40% → 12M
- Africa (~100M): ~15% → 15M
- Other: ~10M

**Total with window access: ~510 million**

**Average daily window-gazing time** (weighted across lifestyles):
| Group | Share | Minutes/day |
|---|---|---|
| Outdoor laborers/farmers | 50% | ~1.5 |
| Indoor workers/housewives | 35% | ~5 |
| Elderly/housebound | 10% | ~10 |
| Upper/leisure class | 5% | ~15 |

**Weighted average ≈ 3.6 minutes/day**

## Calculation

$$510{,}000{,}000 \;\text{people} \times 3.6 \;\text{min/day} \times 365 \;\text{days} \div 1440 \;\text{min/day}$$

$$= \frac{510{,}000{,}000 \times 1{,}314}{1{,}440} \approx 465{,}000{,}000$$

## My estimate: **465,000,000 person-days**

# Estimating Total Human Window-Gazing Days in 1800

## Key Parameters

**World population in 1800:** ~950 million

**Fraction with meaningful window access:**
- **Europe (~200M):** Glass windows were widespread by 1800, even in modest homes → ~65% = 130M
- **East Asia (~370M):** Paper-screen and open-shutter windows in China/Japan → ~12% ≈ 45M
- **South Asia (~200M):** Limited glass/transparent windows → ~8% ≈ 16M
- **Americas, Middle East, Africa (~180M):** Mixed access → ~8% ≈ 15M
- **Total with window access: ~200–210 million**

**Average daily time looking through a window:**
Most people in 1800 were outdoor agricultural laborers with only ~3–5 waking indoor hours. Typical window-gazing: checking weather on waking (~1 min), glances during meals (~2 min), evening gazing (~3–4 min). Urban and wealthier individuals contribute more. I estimate **~7–8 minutes/day** averaged across all window-having people.

## Calculation

$$200{,}000{,}000 \;\text{people} \times 8 \;\text{min/day} \times 365 \;\text{days} \div 1440 \;\text{min/day}$$

$$= 200{,}000{,}000 \times 2.028 \approx 405{,}000{,}000$$

## My Estimate

**450,000,000 days**

(Slightly above the raw calculation to account for underestimation of informal/open-window gazing in warmer climates and children's window behavior.)

## My Estimate

**507,000,000 days**

# Estimating Total Person-Days Spent Looking Through Windows in 1800

## Key Parameters

**World population in 1800:** ~1 billion

**Fraction with meaningful window access (~45%):** ~450 million
- Europe (~200M): Glass windows spreading but many poor had tiny/no glass windows → ~60% = 120M
- China (~330M): Paper windows (very common in traditional architecture) → ~50% = 165M
- Japan (~30M): Shoji screens widespread → ~70% = 21M
- India (~170M): Many homes had openings, some with screens → ~30% = 51M
- Africa, Americas, rest of Asia: lower rates → ~50M combined

**Average daily window-gazing time: ~4 minutes/day**
| Group | % of window-havers | Minutes/day |
|---|---|---|
| Outdoor laborers/farmers | 40% | ~2.5 |
| Women doing domestic work indoors | 25% | ~6 |
| Children (3–15) | 20% | ~4 |
| Elderly/infirm (more idle indoor time) | 5% | ~10 |
| Infants | 10% | ~0.5 |

# Estimating Total Human Window-Gazing Time in 1800

## Key Parameters

**World population in 1800:** ~1,000,000,000

**Fraction with meaningful window access (~45%):**
| Region | Population | Window access rate | People with access |
|---|---|---|---|
| Europe | ~200M | ~70% | ~140M |
| China | ~330M | ~50% | ~165M |
| South Asia | ~200M | ~30% | ~60M |
| Japan | ~30M | ~60% | ~18M |
| Middle East/Africa/Americas | ~240M | ~20% | ~48M |
| **Total** | | | **~430–450M** |

Many civilizations had paper, shuttered, or open windows — not just glass.

**Average daily window-gazing (~7 minutes/day):**
- Agricultural workers (majority): ~5 min (brief glances morning/evening)
- Women working indoors (spinning, cooking near window light): ~10–15 min
- Elderly/sick/children indoors: ~10–20 min
- Wealthy/urban artisans: ~15–20 min
- Weighted average across all types: **~7 minutes/day**

Windows were essential for light (no electricity), so indoor tasks were done *near* windows.

## Calculation

$$450{,}000{,}000 \text{ people} \times 7 \text{ min/day} \times 365 \text{ days} = 1{,}149{,}750{,}000{,}000 \text{ person-minutes}$$

$$\frac{1{,}149{,}750{,}000{,}000}{1{,}440 \text{ min/day}} \approx 798{,}000{,}000 \text{ person-days}$$

## My Estimate

**800,000,000 days**

# Estimating Total Human Window-Looking Time in 1800

## Key Parameters

| SE Asia/Oceania | ~50M | ~30% | 15M |

Most people were agricultural laborers outdoors most of the day, but would check weather, glance out during meals/rest, watch for visitors, etc. Europeans averaged slightly more (~6–8 min) due to colder climates and more indoor time; tropical populations less (~3 min).

= 460,000,000 × 1.27 person-days/year

≈ **583,000,000**

**550,000,000 days**

# Estimating Total Human Window-Gazing Time in 1800

## Key Parameters

**World population in 1800:** ~1 billion

**People with access to windows (broadly defined, including paper/lattice/shuttered openings):**
- Europe (~200M): ~170M had windows
- China (~300M): ~200M (paper/lattice windows common)
- India/S. Asia (~180M): ~80M
- Japan/Korea (~35M): ~25M
- Rest of world: ~25M
- **Total: ~500 million**

**Average daily window-gazing time per person with access:**
- Most people were agricultural laborers, spending most daylight outdoors → ~2 min/day
- Women doing domestic work, elderly, children, craftspeople → ~5–8 min/day
- Weighted average across all groups: **~3.5 minutes/day**

## Calculation

$$500{,}000{,}000 \;\text{people} \times \frac{3.5 \;\text{min/day}}{1440 \;\text{min/day}} \times 365 \;\text{days} \approx 443{,}000{,}000 \;\text{days}$$

## My Estimate

$$\boxed{450{,}000{,}000 \text{ days}}$$

This falls below 500 million. The main driver of uncertainty is the average gazing duration — even shifting from 3.5 to 5 minutes/day would push the estimate above 600 million — but I believe 3.5 minutes best reflects how most of humanity in 1800 (predominantly outdoor laborers with limited indoor leisure time and no artificial lighting extending evenings) actually interacted with windows.

# Estimating Total Person-Days Looking Through Windows in 1800

## Key Parameters

**World population in 1800:** ~950 million

**Fraction with meaningful window access (~45%):**
- **Europe (~200M):** ~70% had glass or functional windows → 140M
- **China (~330M):** Paper/lattice windows were common → ~50% → 165M
- **Indian subcontinent (~180M):** Many dwellings had openings → ~35% → 63M
- **Japan (~30M):** Paper shōji windows → ~50% → 15M
- **Americas, Middle East, Africa, SE Asia (~210M):** Mixed → ~20–30% → ~50M
- **Total with windows: ~430 million**

**Average daily window-gazing time (~8 minutes):**
- Farmers (majority): ~5 min (glancing at weather, brief indoor moments)
- Domestic workers/housewives: ~15 min (working near windows for light)
- Urban craftworkers: ~10 min
- Children/elderly: ~8–12 min
- Weighted average ≈ **8 minutes/day**

## Calculation

$$430{,}000{,}000 \;\text{people} \times 365\;\text{days} \times \frac{8}{1440}\;\text{(day-fraction)} \approx 872{,}000{,}000$$

## My Estimate

**850,000,000 person-days**

# Estimating Total Human Window-Gazing in 1800

## Key Assumptions and Reasoning

**World Population in 1800:** ~1 billion

| Region | Population | Window Access Rate |
|--------|-----------|-------------------|
| Europe (~200M) | Glass/shuttered windows common | ~70% |
| China (~330M) | Paper/lattice windows widespread | ~50% |
| India (~200M) | Openings common, glass rare | ~40% |
| Africa (~100M) | Many traditional dwellings lack windows | ~20% |
| Americas & Rest (~170M) | Mixed | ~40% |

**Weighted result: ~500 million people with window access**

**Average daily window-gazing time (~5 minutes/day):**
- Most people (70%+) were agricultural laborers, outdoors during daylight, with only ~4–5 waking indoor hours → ~3 min/day
- Urban dwellers (~6%) had more indoor time → ~10 min/day
- Elderly/homebound spent significant time indoors → ~15 min/day
- Pre-electric lighting meant people naturally oriented toward windows, but windows were small and often not fully transparent

## Calculation

$$500{,}000{,}000 \text{ people} \times 5 \text{ min/day} \times 365 \text{ days} = 912.5 \text{ billion person-minutes}$$

$$\frac{912{,}500{,}000{,}000}{1{,}440 \text{ min/day}} \approx 634{,}000{,}000 \text{ person-days}$$

## My Estimate

**640,000,000 days**

# Estimating Total Human Window-Gazing Time in 1800

## Key Parameters

**World population in 1800:** ~1 billion

**Fraction with meaningful window access (~45%):**
| Region | Population | Window access rate | People with windows |
|---|---|---|---|
| Europe | ~200M | ~70% | 140M |
| China | ~330M | ~50% (paper/lattice windows common) | 165M |
| Indian subcontinent | ~200M | ~30% | 60M |
| Japan/Korea | ~35M | ~50% (shoji screens) | 17M |
| Rest of world | ~235M | ~20% | 47M |
| **Total** | | | **~430–450M** |

**Average daily window-gazing time (~5 min/day):**

- Women doing domestic work indoors: somewhat more (~6 min)
- Urban workers/artisans (15%): ~7 min/day
- Wealthy/leisure class (5%): ~15 min/day
- Weighted average: **~5 minutes/day**

## Calculation

$$450{,}000{,}000 \text{ people} \times 5 \text{ min/day} \times 365 \text{ days} \div 1440 \text{ min/day}$$

$$= \frac{821{,}250{,}000{,}000 \text{ person-minutes}}{1440} \approx 570{,}000{,}000 \text{ person-days}$$

## Key Parameters

**World population in 1800:** ~1 billion

**Fraction with access to windows (including glass, paper, or open-frame windows):**
| Region | Population | Window access rate | People with windows |
|---|---|---|---|
| Europe | ~200M | ~80% | 160M |
| China/Japan/Korea | ~370M | ~55% | 200M |
| South Asia | ~200M | ~35% | 70M |
| Africa | ~100M | ~20% | 20M |
| Americas/Other | ~130M | ~35% | 42M |
| **Total** | | | **~490–500M** |

**Average daily window-gazing time per person with access:**
- Most people were agricultural workers, spending ~10 hours outdoors in daylight
- ~4–6 indoor daylight hours on average across seasons
- Of ~300 minutes indoors in daylight, roughly 1.5% spent gazing through a window → **~4.5 minutes/day**
- This accounts for brief weather-checks, watching passersby, idle moments, etc.

## Calculation

$$500{,}000{,}000 \text{ people} \times 365 \text{ days} \times \frac{4.5 \text{ min}}{1440 \text{ min/day}} \approx 570{,}000{,}000 \text{ person-days}$$

## My estimate: **570,000,000 days**

# Estimating Total Human Window-Gazing Time in 1800

## Key Parameters

**World population in 1800:** ~1 billion

**Fraction with access to windows (any opening in a wall for light/air):**
- Europe (~200M): ~75% had meaningful window access
- China (~330M): ~45% (lattice/paper windows common in permanent structures)
- India (~170M): ~40%
- Africa (~100M): ~25%
- Americas (~30M): ~50%
- Elsewhere: ~30%

**→ ~500 million people with some window access**

**Average daily window-gazing time (for those with access):**
- Most people were agricultural workers spending daylight hours outdoors
- Indoor time was limited; but tasks like sewing/cooking were done near windows for light
- Quick glances (checking weather, idle gazing, passing by): ~2–5 min/day
- Elderly, urban workers, domestic workers skew higher; field laborers skew lower

**→ ~3.5 minutes/day average**

## Calculation

$$500{,}000{,}000 \text{ people} \times 3.5 \text{ min/day} \times 365 \text{ days} = 638{,}750{,}000{,}000 \text{ person-minutes}$$

$$\frac{638{,}750{,}000{,}000}{1{,}440 \text{ min/day}} \approx 443{,}000{,}000 \text{ person-days}$$

## My Estimate

**450,000,000 person-days**

# Estimating Total Window-Gazing Time in 1800

## Key Assumptions

**World population in 1800:** ~1 billion

**Fraction with access to windows (~40–45%):**
- **China (~330M):** Paper/lattice windows common → ~55% access
- **Europe (~200M):** Glass windows widespread → ~75%
- **India (~200M):** Window-like openings in many homes → ~35%
- **Africa (~100M):** Fewer windowed structures → ~20%
- **Americas & rest (~170M):** Mixed → ~35%

**Weighted estimate: ~45% → ~450 million people with window access**

**Average daily window-gazing time (~4 minutes/day):**
| Group | % of window-havers | Avg gazing | Weighted |
|---|---|---|---|
| Farmers/outdoor laborers | 60% | 2 min | 1.2 min |
| Homemakers/indoor workers | 15% | 10 min | 1.5 min |
| Children (non-working) | 15% | 3 min | 0.45 min |
| Elderly/infirm | 5% | 15 min | 0.75 min |
| Urban craftsmen | 5% | 5 min | 0.25 min |

**Weighted average ≈ 4 minutes/day**

## Calculation

$$450{,}000{,}000 \;\text{people} \times \frac{4 \;\text{min/day}}{1440 \;\text{min/day}} \times 365 \;\text{days} \approx 456{,}000{,}000 \;\text{person-days}$$

## My Estimate

$$\boxed{460{,}000{,}000 \text{ days}}$$

This reflects that the vast majority of people in 1800 worked outdoors for most of daylight hours, windows were small and often opaque, and there was no reason to gaze out after dark (no street lighting). The number is large simply because of the sheer population size.

**World population (1800):** ~1 billion

| India/S. Asia | ~190M | ~35% | 67M |

| Middle East | ~30M | ~35% | 11M |
| Africa & other | ~225M | ~15% | 34M |
| **Total** | | | **~385M** |

**Average daily window-gazing time (~4 minutes/day):**
- Agricultural workers (vast majority): ~2–3 min (checking weather, brief glances at meals)
- Urban workers/craftspeople: ~5 min
- Wealthier/leisured class: ~15 min
- Children and elderly at home: ~8 min
- Weighted average: **~4 min/day**

$$\frac{562{,}100{,}000{,}000}{1{,}440 \text{ min/day}} \approx 390{,}000{,}000 \text{ person-days}$$

# Estimating Total Window-Gazing Time in 1800

## Key Parameters

**World population in 1800:** ~1 billion

**Fraction with access to windows:**
| Region | Population | Window access rate | People with windows |
|---|---|---|---|
| Europe | ~200M | ~75% | 150M |
| China | ~330M | ~55% (paper/lattice windows common) | 180M |
| South Asia | ~200M | ~30% | 60M |
| Japan | ~30M | ~60% | 18M |
| Middle East | ~25M | ~50% | 13M |
| Africa | ~100M | ~15% | 15M |
| Americas/other | ~65M | ~30% | 20M |
| **Total** | | | **~450 million** |

**Average daily window-gazing time (for those with windows):**
- ~80% farmers/laborers (mostly outdoors): ~2.5 min/day
- ~15% urban workers/tradespeople: ~6 min/day
- ~5% upper/middle class, elderly, young children: ~15 min/day
- **Weighted average ≈ 5 minutes/day**

## Calculation

$$450{,}000{,}000 \;\text{people} \times 365 \;\text{days} \times 5 \;\text{min/day} = 821{,}250{,}000{,}000 \;\text{person-minutes}$$

$$\frac{821{,}250{,}000{,}000}{1{,}440 \;\text{min/day}} \approx 570{,}000{,}000 \;\text{days}$$

## My estimate: **570,000,000 days**

# Estimating Total Human Window-Gazing in 1800

## Key Parameters

**World population in 1800:** ~1 billion

**Fraction with meaningful window access (~50%):**
- Europe (~200M): ~75% had windows → 150M
- China (~300M): traditional lattice/paper windows were common, ~50% → 150M
- India (~200M): many homes had openings/jali windows, ~40% → 80M
- Americas, Middle East, Africa, rest (~300M): ~40% → 120M
- **Total: ~500 million people**

**Average daily minutes looking through a window:**
| Group | Share | Minutes/day | Contribution |
|---|---|---|---|
| Agricultural/outdoor workers | 75% | 3 min | 2.25 |
| Urban/indoor workers | 10% | 12 min | 1.20 |
| Elderly, children, homebound | 15% | 8 min | 1.20 |
| **Weighted average** | | | **~4.7 min** |

I'll use **5 minutes/day** — accounting for weather-checking, glancing out while doing indoor tasks near windows for light, and idle gazing.

## Calculation

$$500{,}000{,}000 \text{ people} \times 5 \text{ min/day} \times 365 \text{ days} \div 1440 \text{ min/day}$$

$$= \frac{912{,}500{,}000{,}000}{1{,}440} \approx 634{,}000{,}000 \text{ person-days}$$

## My Estimate

**630,000,000 days**

# Estimating Total Human Window-Gazing in 1800

## Key Parameters

**World population in 1800:** ~1 billion

**Fraction with meaningful window access (~35%):**
| Region | Population | Window access % | People with access |
|---|---|---|---|
| Europe | ~200M | ~50% | 100M |
| East Asia (China/Japan/Korea) | ~350M | ~40% (paper/wooden windows common) | 140M |
| South Asia | ~200M | ~25% | 50M |
| Rest of world | ~250M | ~10–15% | 30M |
| **Total** | | | **~320M** |

**Average daily gazing time (~6–7 minutes/day):**
- ~60% outdoor agricultural workers: **~3 min/day** (brief morning/meal glances)
- ~15% indoor workers/craftsmen: **~12 min/day**
- ~15% homemakers/elderly: **~15 min/day**
- ~10% children/other: **~5 min/day**
- **Weighted average ≈ 6.5 minutes/day**

## Calculation

$$320{,}000{,}000 \text{ people} \times \frac{6.5 \text{ min}}{1440 \text{ min/day}} \times 365 \text{ days/year}$$

$$= 320{,}000{,}000 \times 1.648 \approx 527{,}000{,}000$$

## My estimate: **550,000,000 days**

# Estimating Total Human Window-Gazing Time in 1800

## Key Parameters

**World Population (1800):** ~950 million

**Fraction with meaningful window access (~55%):**
| Region | Population | Window access | People with windows |
|--------|-----------|---------------|-------------------|
| Europe | ~195M | ~85% (glass common) | ~166M |
| China | ~330M | ~50% effective (paper windows, latticed, openable) | ~165M |
| India/S. Asia | ~185M | ~35% | ~65M |
| Japan | ~27M | ~60% (shoji, openable) | ~16M |
| Other regions | ~213M | ~15–45% | ~50M |
| **Total** | | | **~520M** |

**Average daily window-gazing time (~4 minutes/day):**
- ~80% agricultural workers (outdoors most of the day): ~3 min/day
- ~15% urban/indoor workers, elderly, ill: ~8 min/day
- ~10% infants/toddlers: ~0 min/day
- Weighted average ≈ **4 minutes/day**

## Calculation

# Estimating Total Human Window-Gazing in 1800

## Key Parameters

### World Population in 1800
Approximately **1 billion** people.

### Fraction with Access to Windows
Not all had glass windows, but many had openable shutters, paper screens, or framed openings:

| Region | Population | % with windows | People |
|---|---|---|---|
| Europe | ~200M | ~70% | 140M |
| China/East Asia | ~365M | ~35% | 128M |
| South Asia | ~200M | ~25% | 50M |
| Middle East/C. Asia | ~30M | ~40% | 12M |
| Americas | ~30M | ~30% | 9M |
| Africa | ~100M | ~10% | 10M |
| Other | ~75M | ~25% | 19M |
| **Total** | | | **~450M** |

### Average Daily Time Looking Through a Window
- ~75% agricultural/outdoor workers: ~2–3 min/day (brief morning/evening glances)
- ~25% indoor/domestic workers, elderly, children at home: ~8–10 min/day

**Weighted average ≈ 4.5 minutes/day** among those with window access.

## Calculation

$$450{,}000{,}000 \;\text{people} \times 4.5 \;\text{min/day} \times 365 \;\text{days} \div 1440 \;\text{min/day}$$

$$= \frac{450{,}000{,}000 \times 1{,}642.5}{1{,}440} \approx 513{,}000{,}000 \;\text{days}$$

## My Estimate

**510,000,000 days**

# Estimating Total Human Window-Gazing Days in 1800

## Key Parameters

**World Population (1800):** ~1 billion

**Fraction with access to windows (broadly: glass, paper, or open shuttered openings):**
| Region | Population | % with windows | People with access |
|---|---|---|---|
| Europe | ~200M | ~80% | ~160M |
| China | ~330M | ~50% | ~165M |
| India/S. Asia | ~170M | ~30% | ~51M |
| Middle East | ~30M | ~40% | ~12M |
| Americas | ~30M | ~30% | ~9M |
| Africa/Other | ~240M | ~15% | ~36M |
| **Total** | | | **~430–500M** |

I'll use **~480 million**.

**Average daily window-gazing time** (weighted across demographics):
- Most people were agricultural workers spending daylight hours *outdoors* → ~3 min/day
- Women/homemakers spent more time indoors → ~7 min/day
- Children, elderly, infants → ~1–3 min/day
- **Weighted average ≈ 3.5 minutes/day**

# Estimating Total Window-Gazing Days in 1800

## Key Parameters

**World population in 1800:** ~1 billion

**Fraction with window access:**
| Region | Population | Window access rate | People with windows |
|---|---|---|---|
| Europe | ~200M | ~70% | ~140M |
| China/Japan | ~330M | ~40% (paper/lattice windows) | ~135M |
| India | ~200M | ~25% | ~50M |
| Americas | ~30M | ~40% | ~12M |
| Middle East | ~30M | ~30% | ~9M |
| Africa/Other | ~210M | ~5–10% | ~15M |
| **Total** | **1B** | | **~360M** |

**Average daily window-gazing time (for those with windows):**
- Most people were agricultural workers, outdoors 10+ hours/day
- But mornings, meals, evenings, bad weather → indoor time near windows
- Pre-electricity era: looking out the window was a primary form of passive observation
- Urban dwellers: ~10 min/day; rural with windows: ~3–5 min/day
- **Weighted average: ~5 min/day**

Those *without* proper windows: ~0.3 min/day

## Calculation

- **With windows:** 360M × 5 min × 365 days ÷ 1,440 min/day ≈ **456M person-days**
- **Without windows:** 640M × 0.3 min × 365 ÷ 1,440 ≈ **49M person-days**

## My Estimate

answer:
# Estimating Total Window-Gazing Time in 1800

## Key Parameters

**World population in 1800:** ~1 billion (well-established)

**Fraction with access to windows (~50%):**
- **Europe (~200M):** ~75% had windows (glass or shuttered openings) → 150M
- **East Asia (~400M):** Traditional Chinese/Japanese homes widely used paper/lattice windows → ~55% → 220M
- **South Asia (~170M):** Many basic dwellings, but openings were common → ~40% → 68M
- **Africa (~100M):** Many traditional structures had minimal openings → ~15% → 15M
- **Middle East, Americas, other (~130M):** Mixed → ~35% → 47M

**Total with window access: ~500 million**

**Average daily window-gazing (~4 minutes/day):**
- Working adults (60%): Outdoors most of the day, brief glances at meals → ~3 min
- Children (25%): Curious but also outdoors → ~4 min
- Elderly/infirm/domestic workers (15%): More indoor time, windows as "entertainment" → ~10 min
- Weighted average: 0.6×3 + 0.25×4 + 0.15×10 ≈ **4.3 minutes/day**

## Calculation

$$500{,}000{,}000 \text{ people} \times \frac{4 \text{ min/day}}{1440 \text{ min/day}} \times 365 \text{ days} \approx 507{,}000{,}000 \text{ days}$$

## My Estimate

**507,000,000 days**

answer:
# Estimating Total Human Window-Gazing in 1800

## Key Parameters

**World population in 1800:** ~1 billion

**People with meaningful window access (~35%):** ~350 million
| Region | Population | % with windows | People |
|---|---|---|---|
| Europe | ~200M | ~70% | 140M |
| China | ~320M | ~40% (paper/lattice windows common) | 128M |
| India/S. Asia | ~190M | ~25% | 48M |
| Americas | ~30M | ~50% | 15M |
| Africa/other | ~160M | ~12% | 19M |

I excluded infants/toddlers (~10%) who wouldn't meaningfully gaze through windows.

**Average daily window-gazing time: ~6 minutes/day**
- Most people were agrarian, spending daylight hours *outside*
- Indoor time (mornings, evenings, winter, rainy days) offered some window-gazing
- Urban/wealthier people: ~10–15 min/day
- Working rural adults: ~3–4 min/day
- Homebound elderly/sick: ~20+ min/day
- Weighted average: ~6 minutes/day

## Calculation

$$350{,}000{,}000 \text{ people} \times 365 \text{ days} \times \frac{6 \text{ min}}{1440 \text{ min/day}} \approx 532{,}000{,}000 \text{ person-days}$$

## My estimate: **530,000,000 days**
